description_long: This is a well-used axe featuring a long, slightly curved wooden handle with visible wear and discoloration, suggesting frequent handling. The handle transitions from a dark, possibly rubberized grip at the end to a lighter, yellowish wood near the head. The axe head is robust and metallic, painted red with areas of paint chipping and exposed metal, indicating heavy use. The blade is broad and wedge-shaped, with a flat poll on the opposite side, suitable for splitting or chopping. The mounting of the head to the handle appears traditional, with the head wedged onto the handle. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements but some faded markings on the head.
description: A worn axe with a curved wooden handle and a red, chipped metal head, showing signs of heavy use. The handle has a dark grip and the blade is broad and wedge-shaped.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handle and the red, wedge-shaped axe head.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the thickness of the blade and the curvature of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's wear and the broad face of the axe head.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat poll of the axe head and the end of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a dark, possibly rubberized grip at the end; oval cross-section; ergonomic for gripping.
attribute_blade_design: Broad, wedge-shaped steel blade with a single cutting edge and a flat poll; painted red with visible wear.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the head fitted onto the handle.
attribute_visible_markings_or_labels: possible faint text or markings on the blade
